confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/educational video demonstrating scissor palming technique for laparoscopic surgery, not an actual FLS task performance. The video includes title cards, equipment setup, narrated technique demonstration with close-ups, and a summary slide. It is explicitly labeled as 'Trauma Series - Palming the scissor - Part 1' and does not contain an actual suture placement or knot-tying performance on the Penrose drain model. Therefore, task-specific scoring is not applicable.
cannot_determine: Actual task completion time - no performance executed
Suture placement accuracy - no suture passed through drain marks
Knot quality or hand switching - no knot tied
Drain assessment - no functional test of closure performed
technique_summary: This is an educational instructional video focusing on proper scissor palming technique for laparoscopic manipulation. The video demonstrates the mechanical steps of rotating the scissors in and out of the palm while maintaining control, which is a foundational skill for intracorporeal work but not the full FLS task5 assessment.
strengths: Clear, step-by-step demonstration of palming motion
Multiple camera angles showing hand position and rotation

demonstrated_techniques: Index finger mounting/dismounting of scissors ring
Maintaining control during rapid position changes
verbal_cues: Use your thumb to rotate the scissors out and distally
Thumb mounts the remaining ring of the scissors allowing you to cut
Thumb disengages and index finger rotates the scissors back into the palm
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding loss of control during palming transition
Ensuring thumb fully disengages before rotation
Maintaining stability of scissors during rapid exchange

improvement_suggestions: Video is instructional only; not applicable to individual performance scoring
